76022.subject: What Would a Stripe + PayPal Merger Mean for Crypto?
76022.body:
**gm, Defiers!**

**Here are the biggest news in web3:**

* **[Stripe](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/stripe-s-usd53b-paypal-bid-would-combine-bridge-and-pyusd-under-one-owner)**[ makes a $53B bid for PayPal ](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/stripe-s-usd53b-paypal-bid-would-combine-bridge-and-pyusd-under-one-owner)

* **[The DTCC](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/dtcc-starts-live-tokenized-securities-trades-with-more-than-two-dozen-firms)**[ begins live RWA trades with 24+ firms](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/dtcc-starts-live-tokenized-securities-trades-with-more-than-two-dozen-firms)

* **[Ostium](https://thedefiant.io/news/hacks/ostium-halts-trading-after-oracle-exploit-drains-up-to-usd18m-from-vault)**[ was exploited for ~$18M ](https://thedefiant.io/news/hacks/ostium-halts-trading-after-oracle-exploit-drains-up-to-usd18m-from-vault)

Two of the biggest names in payments have become two of the biggest names in stablecoins. A takeover bid would put both books under one roof.

**Stripe**, with private-equity firm **Advent International**, made an unsolicited [roughly $53 billion bid for PayPal](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/stripe-s-usd53b-paypal-bid-would-combine-bridge-and-pyusd-under-one-owner). If PayPal accepts, the deal would pair Stripe's **Bridge** and Privy stablecoin infrastructure with PayPal's **PYUSD** token and crypto-trading business, consolidating two mainstream payments companies' stablecoin operations into one.

The bid is a bet on where payments are heading. Stripe bought Bridge to own stablecoin rails; PayPal built PYUSD to issue its own dollar. Combining them would create a payments giant that both issues a stablecoin and runs the infrastructure to move it, the kind of vertical stack competitors would need years to assemble.

**Read more below!**

вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”

##### PARTNER

# **The Defiant Exclusive: Invest in High-Potential Startups Like These**

**You get:**

* No cost

How we measure email
opens, and how to opt out if you'd
like.
sorare-27-competition-strucutre 
( https://clicks.sorare.com/f/a/uIz7yuTM8HvSXqiEicUjLQ~~/AAAHahA~/3N2FVQ5ZydZCmuaE4h9TBEOn7M--LIx_Ctx-bjS2LXDSRRxk4J5WwcqesHDEvYY2vJ54nWsjXT7lVkfhv6InjK5eGh570ZaL0LQ1tom2IfLxQznfDVINHwUgsxS275wu1spSKsxP7T4yXVNfpPMwn6cxDSxKpBMYqX3ofHKw_c0~ )
Hello,

We're writing to inform you of an update to our emailing
practices.

Like most online platforms, the emails we send you may include a
tracking pixel: an invisible technical element that tells us
whether an email has been opened. This helps us better tailor the
content and frequency of our communications to what's actually
useful to you.

We want to leave this choice up to you. Whether or not you accept
this tracking, you'll keep receiving our emails as usual, the
only thing that changes is our ability to know whether you've
opened them.

Manage my
preferences  (

76020.subject: рҹҹӘ The Chicago Plan Comeback
76020.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/9f199753-ca31-4e3d-9435-5e12896ecff5/BD_NL_Banner_Heading.png?t=1781793903)
Caption: 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/e60d7cde-4250-41f8-9443-28ed51b80560/divider_transparent.png?t=1774649182)
Caption: 

----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/3bd8a492-ff61-41db-a7f2-c821d481fd72/BD_NL_QuoteCircle.png?t=1781794162)
Caption: 

==вҖңMake money independent of loans.вҖқ==
_вҖ” _==_Irving Fisher_==



----------View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/0c7a8a1e-f64e-4940-af8c-728c30439d7b/image.png?t=1784150831)
Caption: 

# The Chicago Plan Comeback

In the depths of the Great Depression, every idea about money was on the table.

During the 1933 bank holiday, FDR proposed converting every government bond into cash at par вҖ” a singularity of debt monetization, so to speak.

His advisors considered having the Federal Reserve print enough new notes to match all bank deposits, so banks could meet withdrawal demands when they reopened.

How wide open was the Overton Window? A year later, FDR appointed Marriner Eccles to lead the Federal Reserve. Eccles, a self-made banker, had only a high-school education.

The most radical proposal, however, came from the pinnacle of academia. The Chicago Plan, proposed by economists at the University of Chicago, would have ended banking as we know it.

Their idea was to abolish fractional reserve banking.

Frank Knight, a co-author of the Chicago Plan, warned that вҖңimportant evils resultвҖқ when commercial banks are allowed to create money вҖ” "notably the frightful instability of the whole economic system and its periodical collapse in crises."

By combining the creation of money with the extension of credit, the academics argued, fractional-reserve banking tends to inflate bubbles on the way up and incite panic on the way down.

Worse still, we _pay_ the banks to provide this destabilizing service.

вҖңIt is absurd and monstrous for society to pay the commercial banking system вҖҳinterestвҖҷ for multiplying several fold the quantity of medium of exchange,вҖқ Knight added.

The Chicago Plan would have turned that arrangement upside down: instead of everyone paying banks to create money for us, banks would pay the government to create it for them.

That, at least, is how the authors of an [IMF paper](https://www.fairmoney.info/wp-content/uploads/2016/12/IMF-The-Chicago-Plan-revisited.pdf?utm_source=chatgpt.com) from 2012 envisioned the Chicago Plan playing out: If banks suddenly needed government-issued money to back all the deposits they had created, theyвҖҷd have to borrow it from its source: the go
